repo_prior_art.cite: none; CausalSmith API has no ExactID QTT/CIC--Callaway--Li equality entry

published_prior_art.cite: AtheyImbens2006 and CallawayLi2019 supply the two maps; equality when the transports are assumed to coincide is routine

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dist_synth_control_v1 is adjacent distributional/coupling work, not a staggered CIC--Callaway--Li QTT frontier

published_prior_art.cite: AtheyImbens2006; CallawayLi2019; LiLin2024: no published finite-support iff equality frontier found

repo_prior_art.cite: q_did_drdid_ipwdid_frontier_v1 and panel_sdid_mc_id_v1 are mean-estimator frontiers, not nonlinear QTT sign wedges

published_prior_art.cite: GoodmanBacon2021/SunAbraham2021/deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020 are mean-DID contamination analogues only; no exact QTT sign-wedge result found

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8 flagship block cites AtheyImbens2006, CallawayLi2019, and LiLin2024 without a location-level anchor to the exact equality-frontier gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the sign-wedge target does not name a published staggered-QTT workflow that treats CIC and Callaway--Li plug-in signs as interchangeable robustness checks.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8 comparison to GoodmanBacon2021, SunAbraham2021, and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020 is by bibkey only and those papers are mean-DID analogues, not anchored nonlinear-QTT targets.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The condition that the CIC rank transport and Callaway--Li copula transport 'coincide' is not defined as a formal object before the theorem.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumptions finite-support--copula-balance include timing homogeneity, which already forces the timing-skew moment to vanish and collapses the advertised frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The residual C is defined as Q_CL - Q_CIC - M, so under timing homogeneity the iff reduces to equality iff the equality residual is zero.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumptions finite-support--margin include timing homogeneity M=0 and also margin |M| >= 4 epsilon with epsilon > 0, so the stated assumption set is inconsistent.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The concrete witness uses cohort h=2 in R_{1,2}, but the proposal defines R_{g,t}={h:h>t}; at t=2 cohort 2 is already treated and is not in the risk set.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Restate Conjecture 1 under assumptions that do not include timing homogeneity; make timing homogeneity only the zero-moment corollary.
Replace C with an independently defined copula/rank imbalance term, or state the result as a decomposition instead of an iff using a residual that contains the estimator difference.
Fix the non-corner witness by using a valid not-yet-treated risk set, e.g. a cell where both cohort 2 and never-treated units are untreated, or redefine risk sets precisely.
Name a published empirical or software workflow that reports CIC and Callaway--Li QTT as robustness checks, then phrase Conjecture 2 as a strict correction to that workflow.

recommended_next_step: Revise the assumption ranges and risk-set definitions first; then add location-anchored comparator claims before re-requesting flagship review.
